title: Guillauminia albiflora
intro: Guillauminia albiflora is a plant species within the family Rubiaceae, commonly found in the tropical regions of Madagascar. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it beneficial for the treatment of wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The plant's bioactive constituents include iridoid glycosides, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. Guillauminia albiflora is often used in traditional medicine and is also incorporated into herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Guillauminia albiflora is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This plant is native to the island of New Caledonia. The classification of Guillauminia albiflora is as follows: Kingdom - Plantae, Clade - Angiosperms, Clade - Eudicots, Clade - Asterids, Order - Gentianales, Family - Rubiaceae. The genus Guillauminia is named after the French botanist André Guillaumin.
